narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD weakness because weakening US data, fading D1 CSM to zero, and DXY stagnation at 98.45 create a structural headwind for the dollar across all majors.
2. JPY cross momentum because EURJPY, GBPJPY, CADJPY, and AUDJPY all show H4 Strong Buy signals with correlation clusters above 0.90, confirming yen selling is the dominant intraday theme.
3. Risk-on sentiment because VIX falling 5.9% to 17.21, S&P500 up 0.9%, and copper surging 3.3% collectively point to broad risk appetite driving commodity and growth-linked currencies higher.

PRICE CONFIRMS

GBPJPY is the lead instrument, carrying the strongest H4 signal at plus four and sitting within a correlation cluster where EURJPY and CADJPY both confirm at plus 0.96 to 0.98, removing noise from the read. AUDUSD is the lead G10 spot pair, with AUD H4 CSM accelerating to 87 against a D1 reading of 32, and RBA policy divergence providing the fundamental anchor. NZDUSD lags AUDUSD as expected given the 0.88 correlation, and NZD H4 CSM at 100 suggests catch-up potential rather than a leading signal.

REGIME

Risk-On confirmed. VIX at 17.21 and falling hard removes the fear premium, S&P500 at record levels with a 0.9% daily gain validates equity risk appetite, but gold rising 1.1% to 4570 introduces a mild conflict suggesting residual safe-haven demand that prevents a clean risk-on call.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. Gold versus risk-on regime: gold up 1.1% simultaneously with VIX down and equities higher suggests USD distrust rather than fear, muddying the pure risk-on signal.
2. USD CSM divergence: D1 CSM shows USD at 100 strength while H4 CSM has collapsed to zero, meaning the daily structural trend has not yet fully validated the intraday USD selling, creating potential for short-covering.

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: GBPJPY up - H4 strong buy, 0.98 EURJPY correlation confirms, risk-on backdrop supports - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: AUDUSD up - RBA divergence, AUD H4 CSM accelerating at 87, copper surge aligns - Conviction 7/10
Avoid: USDCHF - CHF H4 CSM at 96 accelerating against USD fading creates unpredictable squeeze risk
Watch: DXY breaking below 98.00 confirms USD H4 CSM collapse is real and triggers the primary trade with full conviction.

themes.theme: Oil shock and Middle East ceasefire easing risk premium
Weakening US economic data and lower yields pressuring USD
RBA monetary policy divergence supporting AUD
ECB hawkish stance limiting EUR downside
Commodity prices volatile on geopolitical and demand shifts
US equity strength despite economic data weakness

key_observation: USD weakening across major pairs as oil shock eases geopolitical risk premium, US economic data deteriorates with lower job openings, and yield support diminishes. Meanwhile, RBA hawkishness supports AUD and rate differentials favor GBP and commodity currencies. Risk-on sentiment emerges with Middle East ceasefire holding and equity markets stabilizing.
watch: USD/JPY intervention threat, AUD/USD RBA guidance, EUR/USD range persistence, crude oil price action, US yield trajectory, corporate earnings quality given market strength on weak macro data
